subject: Geology
title: Non-Silicate Minerals
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Non-Silicate Minerals.

hook.text: Gold, halite, calcite, and magnetite have nothing to do with silicon. They belong to a different mineral world.

buildup.text: Non-silicate minerals include carbonates, oxides, sulfides, halides, and native elements like copper and gold.

discovery.text: Calcite builds limestone and marble. Hematite provides iron. Halite is table salt. Each group has vital uses.

twist.text: Though they make up less than 10% of the crust, non-silicates are often more economically valuable than silicates.

climax.text: Non-silicates may be rare in the crust, but civilization depends on mining them for metals, salts, and gems.

punchline.text: The rarest minerals are often the most useful.

quiz.question: Which of these is a non-silicate mineral?
